mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to separate bull and bear regimes with a t-statistic greater than 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative median forward returns, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.01, or returns are consistently negative during recovery phases despite rising production.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL surprises yield positive forward returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows negative correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates sustained aggregate demand growth, reinforcing earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative median returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of instances, or the signal fails to achieve statistical significance at the 5% level.
